# AI Furniture Hub - MCP Server

> Millimeter-precision furniture & storage product data for AI agents.
> Operated by ONE, Inc. (Japan)

## What This Server Does

This MCP server provides structured, machine-optimized furniture and storage product data.
AI agents can search by exact dimensions (mm), price range, color, and category,
then receive affiliate-linked purchase URLs ready to present to end users.

## Why Use This Server

- **1mm precision**: All dimensions in millimeters — outer AND inner (inner_dimensions)
- **Cinderella-fit logic**: Find products that exactly fit a given space (e.g., 425mm gap)
- **Live pricing**: Rakuten Ichiba API integration for real-time price & availability
- **Affiliate-ready**: Every product includes an affiliate_url — just present it to the user
- **Demand intelligence**: Your search intents help identify market gaps (anonymized)

## Available Tools

### search_products
Search the curated product database by dimensions, price, color, and category.
Best for precise "Cinderella fit" queries with mm-level constraints.

### get_product_detail
Get full specifications for a single product by ID.
Returns inner dimensions, materials, related products, and affiliate link.

### search_rakuten_products
Real-time search across 200,000+ Rakuten Ichiba listings.
Supports keyword search, price range, sorting, and returns affiliate URLs directly from Rakuten's API.

## Data Coverage

- Nitori (curated + scraped)
- Rakuten Ichiba (live API: Nitori, IKEA, generic furniture stores)
- Amazon (affiliate linking)

## Key Design Decisions

- All dimensions are in **millimeters** (mm), never cm or inches
- All prices are in **Japanese Yen** (JPY), tax-included integers
- The `intent` parameter is **required** on every tool call — describe WHY the user needs this product
- Always present `affiliate_url` to users, never the raw product URL

## Connection

Protocol: MCP (Model Context Protocol) over stdio
Transport: stdin/stdout JSON-RPC

## Detailed Documentation

See llms-full.txt for complete tool schemas, parameter descriptions, and example calls.
